blurb: Law-firm AI/regulatory practice. Launch coverage uses a named first-party EU AI Act publication; body recapture needs browser due 403.

publications.title: The EU AI Act: Prohibited practices and AI literacy requirements take effect
publications.summary: Hogan Lovells explains the first EU AI Act obligations taking effect in February 2025: prohibited AI practices and AI-literacy requirements for companies.

publications.title: EU legislators agree to delay for high-risk AI rules
publications.summary: Hogan Lovells reports the Council/Parliament provisional agreement to delay high-risk AI Act obligations under the Digital Omnibus.

publications.title: European Commission publishes long-awaited guidelines on high-risk AI systems
publications.summary: Hogan Lovells analyses the Commission draft guidelines on classification of high-risk AI systems under the AI Act.

notes:
launch_harvest 2026-06-27: first-party URL discovered on official domain; direct fetch returned HTTP 403, so status=needs_browser for full-body recapture.

2026-07-02 urgent data-quality pass: added two first-party Hogan Lovells EU AI Act items; final URLs verified via web_fetch redirecting to hlc.com; bare fetch/curl can be user-agent sensitive, but browser-like fetch returned HTTP 200. No controversy logged.
